name: colrm
readme:
Short:        Remove columns from lines (OpenBSD)
Type:         util/cli
Architecture: m68k-amigaos >= 3.0
Uploader:     duncan (at) bowring (dot) us
Author:       The Regents of the University of California (ported by Duncan Bowring)
Version:      1.14

colrm - Remove columns from lines (OpenBSD)

Original author: The Regents of the University of California
Ported by Duncan Bowring using amiport (AI-assisted porting toolkit).
Project: https://github.com/bdgscotland/amiport

Cross-compiled with m68k-amigaos-gcc for AmigaOS 3.x (68000+).
Tested in FS-UAE with AmigaOS 3.1.

Usage:
  colrm [startcol [endcol]]

  Removes columns from lines of input. Column numbering starts at 1.
  With one argument, removes from startcol to end of line.
  With two arguments, removes from startcol through endcol.

Examples:
  colrm 10            ; remove from column 10 to end of each line
  colrm 5 10          ; remove columns 5 through 10
  colrm 1 3 <file     ; remove first 3 columns from file

Includes full source code (original POSIX and ported Amiga versions)
and PORT.md documenting every transformation applied.
porting_notes: Standard Tier 1 port. 28 tests cover one-arg and two-arg modes, empty input, single-character lines, tab handling, stress cases with long lines and varying column positions, and edge cases at column boundaries.
